risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The randomization list and numbered packing of the intervention, allocating patient in 1:1 ratio to either SCT or non SCT groups, were prepared off site by central data coordinator, for all centres. The random numbers were generated by a computer programme using permuted blocks of variable length." Comment: This describes a clearly random method (computer-generated random numbers) with permuted blocks, prepared off site by a central coordinator, which is sufficient to judge the sequence generation as truly random.

_evidence.method_quote: The randomization list and numbered packing of the intervention, allocating patient in 1:1 ratio to either SCT or non SCT groups, were prepared off site by central data coordinator, for all centres. The random numbers were generated by a computer programme using permuted blocks of variable length.
_evidence.method_summary: Random numbers were computer-generated using permuted blocks of variable length, with allocation prepared off site by a central data coordinator.
context_clues: prepared off site by central data coordinator

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The randomization list and numbered packing of the intervention, allocating patient in 1:1 ratio to either SCT or non SCT groups, were prepared off site by central data coordinator, for all centres." Comment: This explicitly describes an off-site central data coordinator preparing the randomization list and numbered intervention packing, which indicates a central allocation mechanism that prevented recruiters from foreseeing the next assignment.

_evidence.concealment_quote: The randomization list and numbered packing of the intervention, allocating patient in 1:1 ratio to either SCT or non SCT groups, were prepared off site by central data coordinator, for all centres.
_evidence.note: Describes sequence generation and off-site preparation of numbered packing, but does not mention envelopes.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The present clinical trial was a Phase III prospective, open labelled, randomized, multicentric trial" and "The patients randomized to SCT group received intracoronary stem cell infusion". Comment: The study explicitly states it was open labelled and does not describe blinding of participants or personnel; because several outcomes could plausibly be influenced by knowledge of assignment (e.g. hospitalization for chest pain, dyspnoea, adverse events, serious adverse events), this domain is at high risk of performance bias. Although some outcomes were objective, the overall study includes subjective/patient-influenced outcomes, so the dominant risk is high.

_evidence.blinding_quote: The present clinical trial was a Phase III prospective, open labelled, randomized, multicentric trial to assess the efficacy of autologous bone marrow derived mononuclear cells (MNC) on LV function of patients with post ST elevation AMI.
_evidence.blinding_method: open labelled; no placebo or sham described
outcome_categories: hospitalization for chest pain/breathing difficulty/syncope/heart failure/arrhythmias
subjective_outcomes: hospitalization for chest pain/breathing difficulty/syncope/heart failure/arrhythmias

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "An independent external observer, not involved in the study, reviewed MUGA scans at all centers... The nuclear medicine specialists in all centers and the independent external observer were blinded to each other and patient assignment." Comment: The primary outcome (LVEF by MUGA) was assessed by an independent blinded observer, and the outcome measurement is objective. Although some secondary outcomes included hospitalization/symptom-based events, the article explicitly states blinded outcome assessment for the key imaging outcome, and no unblinded subjective outcome assessment is described in a way that would invalidate this domain for detection bias here.

_evidence.assessor_blinding_quote: An independent external observer, not involved in the study, reviewed MUGA scans at all centers. In case of intraobserver variation, the P value by the independent observer was taken as the final value for analysis. The nuclear medicine specialists in all centers and the independent external observer were blinded to each other and patient assignment.

outcomes.label: hospitalization for treatment of chest pain, breathing difficulty, syncope, heart failure or arrhythmias

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The final cohort followed up for six months included, 109 patients in SCT group and 117 in non-SCT group. Significant number of trial deviates were noted (n=61). In the non-SCT group eight were lost to follow up and in the SCT group there were 53 trial deviates..." Comment: The analysed 6-month outcome had follow-up of 117/125 (93.6%) in the non-SCT arm and 109/125 (87.2%) in the SCT arm, with total loss 9.6% and differential loss 6.4 percentage points. Although there were many protocol deviations in the SCT arm, the post-randomization loss on the analysed outcome was under 10% overall, reasonably complete, and the paper also reports ITT analysis for the primary outcome, so this does not suggest important attrition bias.

per_arm_flow.reasons: 53 trial deviates; 114 received the stem cells

_evidence.missing_data_method: intention-to-treat (ITT) as well as per-protocol analysis
_evidence.notes: Final six-month cohort was 109 in SCT and 117 in non-SCT. The 16 losses in SCT are not all classical follow-up losses; the article distinguishes 53 'trial deviates' (received suboptimal cell dose) from 8 lost to follow up in non-SCT.
